title: Ananta Test Coverage and CI Workflow Roadmap
purpose: Track missing tests, coverage improvements and CI workflow split so quality increases without making GitHub Actions too slow or expensive.
principles: Keep push and pull_request CI fast enough for normal development.
Run expensive E2E, chaos, security-abuse and performance tests in separate manual or scheduled workflows.
Prefer deterministic local mocks in normal CI; use real providers only in opt-in smoke workflows.
Never hide important tests by disabling them; isolate heavy or flaky flows with explicit workflow names and timeouts.
Every high-risk autonomous-agent capability needs negative tests, not only happy-path tests.

must_include: release reproducibility gate without image build
must_avoid: large Docker image rebuilds unless cached and necessary
full browser E2E on every small pull request

must_include: optional live LLM smoke with strict token limits

recommended_workflows.name: Quality And Docs
recommended_workflows.purpose: Fast and broad default CI for normal development.
recommended_workflows.notes: Keep this workflow mostly fast. Compose E2E may stay only on main, workflow_dispatch or full-ci label.

recommended_workflows.name: Backend Isolated Flows
recommended_workflows.purpose: Run heavier backend E2E/integration/RAG-load tests separately from the deep sweep.
recommended_workflows.notes: If this gets too slow, split into autonomous-flows, research-flows and webhook-flows.

recommended_workflows.name: Coverage Report
recommended_workflows.purpose: Generate backend and frontend coverage artifacts without slowing every PR.

recommended_workflows.name: Security Abuse Tests
recommended_workflows.purpose: Run prompt-injection, tool-abuse, secret-leak and command-injection tests.

recommended_workflows.name: Full Compose E2E
recommended_workflows.purpose: Run full Docker Compose browser and API E2E with artifacts on failure.

recommended_workflows.name: Chaos And Timeout Tests
recommended_workflows.purpose: Validate failures, timeouts, worker crashes, backend interruptions and partial state recovery.

recommended_workflows.name: Live Provider Smoke
recommended_workflows.purpose: Run one minimal hosted LLM request with strict token and timeout limits.
recommended_workflows.notes: Keep this tiny and opt-in. Do not run broad paid-provider tests on normal PRs.

todo_groups.goal: Add explicit backend and frontend coverage reporting.
todo_groups.why: Green tests show behavior is covered somewhere, but not which source modules are uncovered.

tasks.title: Add pytest-cov backend coverage command
acceptance: CI uploads coverage.xml as artifact
summary shows total backend coverage
coverage job does not block normal PRs initially

tasks.title: Add frontend Vitest coverage command
acceptance: frontend coverage artifact is uploaded
coverage summary is visible in job logs
normal frontend-build remains fast

tasks.title: Introduce coverage thresholds after baseline is known
acceptance: initial thresholds are realistic and do not cause noisy failures
critical modules get higher thresholds than UI glue code

todo_groups.goal: Expand negative tests for autonomous agent safety boundaries.
todo_groups.why: The highest risk is not normal behavior; it is unsafe LLM/tool behavior, privilege bypass and secret leakage.

tasks.title: Prompt injection must not bypass blocked tools
acceptance: LLM output asking to ignore policy is rejected
forbidden shell commands are blocked before execution
policy decision is logged with reason

tasks.title: Secrets must not leak into logs, artifacts or LLM prompts
acceptance: environment-like secrets are redacted
API tokens are redacted
artifact metadata does not expose secret values
LLM prompt construction redacts sensitive fields

tasks.title: Command injection attempts are blocked in shell/sgpt/opencode bridges
acceptance: semicolon and pipe injection are rejected where commands should be structured
path traversal in command arguments is blocked
unsafe generated commands require review or are rejected

tasks.title: SSRF-like URLs are rejected or sandboxed in research and webhook paths
acceptance: localhost/private-network URLs are blocked where external fetch is possible
file:// and metadata-service URLs are blocked
blocked attempts are auditable

todo_groups.goal: Add explicit authorization matrix tests for all important endpoints.
todo_groups.why: Agent platforms need exact role boundaries: anonymous, user, admin, hub, worker.

tasks.title: Generate endpoint x role permission matrix tests
acceptance: anonymous cannot access protected endpoints
user cannot perform admin actions
worker cannot perform hub-only actions
admin-only endpoints are documented by test table

tasks.title: Invalid or escalated token claims are rejected
acceptance: modified role claim fails verification
expired token fails
wrong audience/issuer fails if configured

todo_groups.goal: Test concurrency, leases and duplicate work prevention.
todo_groups.why: Race conditions are likely in multi-agent orchestration and can cause duplicate execution or lost state.

tasks.title: Two workers must not claim the same task
acceptance: parallel claim requests produce exactly one winner
loser receives deterministic conflict/empty result
task history contains one claim event

tasks.title: Expired leases can be reclaimed safely
acceptance: expired lease is not treated as active
new worker can reclaim
old worker completion after expiry is rejected or reconciled

tasks.title: Parallel autopilot ticks do not duplicate dispatch
acceptance: concurrent tick_once calls do not create duplicate execution
locking or idempotency behavior is visible in assertions

todo_groups.goal: Add failure-mode tests for workers, research backends, shell and LLM output.
todo_groups.why: The system must fail closed and recover cleanly from partial failures.

tasks.title: Worker timeout marks task safely and does not hang CI
acceptance: timeout produces bounded failure state
task remains retryable or escalated
no background thread hangs after test

tasks.title: Invalid LLM JSON is handled safely
acceptance: invalid JSON does not crash planner
repair fallback is bounded
unsafe fallback does not execute tools

tasks.title: Research backend timeout is bounded and auditable
acceptance: CLI timeout is enforced
partial artifact is not treated as verified
review remains required after uncertain research result

tasks.title: Shell session crash does not corrupt task state
acceptance: crashed shell marks execution failed
task history includes failure reason
subsequent execution can create a fresh session

todo_groups.goal: Test realistic upgrade scenarios from older persisted states.
todo_groups.why: Users may keep tasks, artifacts, configs and memory entries across releases.

tasks.title: Old config documents upgrade to current config model
acceptance: missing new config keys get safe defaults
old values are preserved
unsafe defaults are not introduced

tasks.title: Old task records still render in current read models
acceptance: old task without new fields does not crash read model
new fields have sensible defaults
frontend contract baseline is still satisfied

tasks.title: Old artifact and memory records remain queryable
acceptance: old artifacts can be listed
old extracted documents can be searched or skipped safely
old memory entries do not break task detail views

todo_groups.goal: Add explicit Playwright golden paths for core user journeys.
todo_groups.why: Frontend unit tests are good, but they do not prove real dashboard flows work in a browser against the stack.

tasks.title: Login, open dashboard, create goal and see resulting tasks
acceptance: user can log in
dashboard loads without console errors
guided goal or quick goal can be submitted
created task appears in UI

tasks.title: Governance and review information is visible for tasks/goals
acceptance: policy decision appears in UI
verification state appears in UI
blocked or review-required state is understandable

tasks.title: Frontend displays useful errors when hub/API is unavailable
acceptance: network failure does not crash UI
user sees actionable error
retry is possible

todo_groups.goal: Keep full-stack and release tests useful without making every PR expensive.
todo_groups.workflow: Full Compose E2E / Nightly And RC Validation

tasks.title: Always upload compose logs on E2E failure
acceptance: docker compose logs are uploaded
frontend Playwright traces are uploaded
backend health endpoint output is included

tasks.title: Keep release image build gate manual or RC-only
acceptance: normal PRs do not build all release images
workflow_dispatch can run full image gate
RC tags run image gate automatically

tasks.title: After GHCR push, verify docker pull and container start
acceptance: docker pull ghcr.io/ananta888/ananta-quickstart-no-ollama:<tag> succeeds
container starts
health endpoint responds

todo_groups.goal: Make test coverage understandable for maintainers and contributors.

tasks.title: Document test categories and when each workflow runs
acceptance: standard CI vs manual CI is documented
expensive workflows are documented
local commands are listed

tasks.title: Add useful CI badges to README
acceptance: Quality And Docs badge exists
Live LLM Smoke badge exists if useful
Backend Isolated Flows badge exists if kept separate

tasks.title: Add CI summary with collected test counts
acceptance: backend-checks prints collected test count
frontend-build prints unit test count
isolated flow workflow prints test count

manual_workflow_candidates.reason: Coverage is useful but can slow CI; run weekly or manually until thresholds are stable.
Security abuse tests may grow large and include many negative scenarios; run nightly or on security-sensitive PRs.
Chaos tests intentionally simulate failures and can be slow/flaky if run on every PR.
Full Docker/browser tests are expensive but necessary before releases and after main merges.
Validates published artifacts and GHCR images after release or RC push.

short_term: All current tests green in CI
Coverage workflow exists and uploads artifacts
Backend isolated flows visible as separate workflow
Security abuse test file group started
medium_term: Backend coverage threshold introduced for critical modules
Frontend golden-path Playwright tests cover first-run and goal creation
Race condition tests cover task claiming and lease expiry
Upgrade tests cover old config and old task records
long_term: Release candidate workflow includes full compose E2E, coverage, security abuse and image pull smoke
Chaos workflow validates failure recovery weekly
Security-sensitive code paths have negative tests as default requirement

notes: Do not move all heavy workflows into the default Quality And Docs workflow. That makes normal development too slow and expensive.
Prefer labels such as full-ci, security-ci, coverage and live-llm for opt-in PR expansion.
Use strict timeouts and faulthandler_timeout for integration tests to avoid silent hangs.
If a test is valuable but slow, isolate it. Do not delete it just to make CI green.
Paid provider tests must use tiny prompts, tiny output limits, one retry at most and explicit secret checks.
